subject: English
title: Comprehension Champion
tagline: Read complex texts and answer inference and literal questions!

stages.name: Stage 1
stages.question: What is 'tone' in a passage?
options: To introduce the main idea of a paragraph
A first-hand account or original document
The author's attitude towards the subject
To create vivid or imaginative meaning
stages.correctOption: The author's attitude towards the subject
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The author's attitude towards the subject is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The author's attitude towards the subject.

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.question: What is 'bias' in a text?
options: A one-sided or unfair view
To introduce the main idea of a paragraph
An opposing point the author responds to
The literal dictionary meaning of a word
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A one-sided or unfair view is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A one-sided or unfair view.

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.question: What is a rhetorical question?
options: Minor details and examples
A one-sided or unfair view
A question asked for effect, not needing an answer
To create vivid or imaginative meaning
stages.correctOption: A question asked for effect, not needing an answer
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A question asked for effect, not needing an answer is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A question asked for effect, not needing an answer.

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.question: What does 'skim reading' help you find?
options: The general idea of a text quickly
An opposing point the author responds to
The feeling or idea a word suggests beyond its meaning
To introduce the main idea of a paragraph
stages.correctOption: The general idea of a text quickly
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The general idea of a text quickly is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The general idea of a text quickly.

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.question: What is a 'counter-argument' in a persuasive text?
options: A one-sided or unfair view
An opposing point the author responds to
A source that interprets or analyses primary sources
To create vivid or imaginative meaning
stages.correctOption: An opposing point the author responds to
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! An opposing point the author responds to is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is An opposing point the author responds to.

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.question: What is 'connotation'?
options: To create vivid or imaginative meaning
The feeling or idea a word suggests beyond its meaning
A source that interprets or analyses primary sources
To introduce the main idea of a paragraph
stages.correctOption: The feeling or idea a word suggests beyond its meaning
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The feeling or idea a word suggests beyond its meaning is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The feeling or idea a word suggests beyond its meaning.

stages.name: Stage 7
stages.question: What is 'denotation'?
options: The author's attitude towards the subject
The literal dictionary meaning of a word
A comparison between two different things
Reading that questions and evaluates the text
stages.correctOption: The literal dictionary meaning of a word
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The literal dictionary meaning of a word is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The literal dictionary meaning of a word.

stages.name: Stage 8
stages.question: What is an 'analogy'?
options: A one-sided or unfair view
The literal dictionary meaning of a word
A comparison between two different things
The author's attitude towards the subject
stages.correctOption: A comparison between two different things
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A comparison between two different things is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A comparison between two different things.

stages.name: Stage 9
stages.question: What is a 'text feature'?
options: Minor details and examples
The feeling or idea a word suggests beyond its meaning
An opposing point the author responds to
Elements like headings and captions that support the text
stages.correctOption: Elements like headings and captions that support the text
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Elements like headings and captions that support the text is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Elements like headings and captions that support the text.

stages.name: Stage 10
stages.question: What is 'critical reading'?
options: A question asked for effect, not needing an answer
Elements like headings and captions that support the text
To create vivid or imaginative meaning
Reading that questions and evaluates the text
stages.correctOption: Reading that questions and evaluates the text
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Reading that questions and evaluates the text is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Reading that questions and evaluates the text.

stages.name: Stage 11

options: Reading that questions and evaluates the text
To create vivid or imaginative meaning
A first-hand account or original document
To introduce the main idea of a paragraph

stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A first-hand account or original document is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A first-hand account or original document.

options: Elements like headings and captions that support the text
The literal dictionary meaning of a word
A source that interprets or analyses primary sources
A one-sided or unfair view
stages.correctOption: A source that interprets or analyses primary sources
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A source that interprets or analyses primary sources is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A source that interprets or analyses primary sources.

stages.name: Stage 13
stages.question: What is the purpose of a topic sentence?
options: The feeling or idea a word suggests beyond its meaning
The general idea of a text quickly
To introduce the main idea of a paragraph
A source that interprets or analyses primary sources

options: The author's attitude towards the subject
Minor details and examples
To create vivid or imaginative meaning
An opposing point the author responds to

stages.correctFeedback: Yes! To create vivid or imaginative meaning is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is To create vivid or imaginative meaning.

stages.name: Stage 15
stages.question: What is a 'summary' expected to leave out?
options: Minor details and examples
The general idea of a text quickly
A first-hand account or original document
The author's attitude towards the subject
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Minor details and examples is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Minor details and examples.
